intro: The biggest real distinction between Cloudflare and Fastly is straightforward: Cloudflare uses freemium with a paid tier from $20/mo (Pro), enterprise plan available; Fastly uses usage-based pricing starting at $50/mo (pay-as-you-go), $0.12/GB, enterprise plan available. Cloudflare sits at freemium with a paid tier from $20/mo (Pro), enterprise plan available and carries moderate lock-in, while Fastly sits at usage-based pricing starting at $50/mo (pay-as-you-go), $0.12/GB, enterprise plan available and carries moderate lock-in. Cloudflare is a fit when you need cdn + edge compute + dns + security in one — typical scenarios include cdn + edge compute + dns + security in one. Fastly is aimed at global, with workloads like global audience with low latency needs as the common case. The honest trade-off: Cloudflare is explicitly not for need full paas for backend apps; Fastly is not intended for internal projects. On cost specifically, pricing model matters more than sticker price — one workload can make Cloudflare cheaper while a different shape flips the answer to Fastly.
quick_take: Cloudflare is for cdn + edge compute + dns; Fastly is for global; decide based on cost model and budget.
verdict_a: Choose Cloudflare if your primary workload is cdn + edge compute + dns + security in one and the free tier plus $20/mo (Pro) paid tier fits your budget.
verdict_b: Choose Fastly if your primary workload is global and you prefer usage-based pricing (starts at $50/mo (pay-as-you-go), $0.12/GB).

faq.a: It depends on workload shape: Cloudflare lists $20/mo (Pro), while Fastly lists $50/mo (pay-as-you-go), $0.12/GB. With different pricing models (freemium vs usage), the cheaper option flips based on seat count, usage volume, and whether you use the free tier. Do a per-workload estimate rather than comparing headline numbers.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Cloudflare to Fastly?
faq.a: Migrating from Cloudflare to Fastly is doable but not automatic — Cloudflare carries moderate lock-in. Since both tools sit in the same category (CDN / Edge / Acceleration), the conceptual mapping is closer, but you still need to validate feature parity against your specific workload. Plan migration as a project, not a script: inventory integrations, test on non-production workloads, and keep Cloudflare running in parallel until Fastly is verified.

Our data gives Cloudflare and Fastly the same developer experience score (5/5), so neither has a clear edge on DX by that measure. The differentiator is usually how each tool fits your workflow — things like CLI quality, docs, local dev loop, and error messages. The safest call is to pilot both on a representative task and see which one gets out of your way.

faq.q: Is Fastly a good alternative to Cloudflare?
faq.a: Fastly is in the same category as Cloudflare (CDN / Edge / Acceleration), so it's a reasonable alternative to evaluate. Fastly is aimed at global, while Cloudflare targets cdn + edge compute + dns + security in one — that difference should drive the switch decision. Note that Fastly is explicitly not for internal projects — if that describes your workload, it's not a viable replacement.
